zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabel De Huntingdon於1212年出生在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓時，她的父親David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon68歲，母親Matilda of Chester41歲。她於1209年與Lord Robert De Bruce在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1251年在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾薩福崔。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Isabel De Huntingdon于1212年出生在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓时，她的父亲David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon68岁，母亲Matilda of Chester41岁。她于1209年与Lord Robert De Bruce在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1251年在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾享年39岁，埋葬在英国英格兰坎布利其爾薩福崔。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell

It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabel De Huntingdon was born in 1212, in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, was 68 and her mother, Matilda of Chester, was 41. She married Lord Robert De Bruce in 1209, in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1251, in Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabel De Huntingdon est née en 1212, à Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, avait 68 ans et sa mère, Matilda of Chester, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé Lord Robert De Bruce en 1209, à Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1251, à Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabel De Huntingdon 1212 in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon 68 und ihre Mutter Matilda of Chester 41. Sie hat 1209 in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom Lord Robert De Bruce geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1251 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel De Huntingdon è nata il nel 1212 nel Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, aveva 68 anni e sua madre, Matilda of Chester, aveva 41 anni. Lei sposato Lord Robert De Bruce nel 1209, nel Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1251, nel Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolta a Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel De Huntingdonが1212年にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアハンティングドンで生まれた時，父David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdonは68歳，母Matilda of Chesterは41歳であった。1209年にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアハンティングドンでLord Robert De Bruceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1251年にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアで39歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアサウトリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel De Huntingdon(이)가 1212년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 헌틴그던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon(은)는 68세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda of Chester(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1209년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 헌틴그던에서 Lord Robert De Bruce(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1251년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 사w트리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel De Huntingdon nasceu em 1212, em Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, tinha 68 anos e sua mãe, Matilda of Chester, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com Lord Robert De Bruce em 1209, em Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1251, em Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabel De Huntingdon родилась в 1212 г. в местности хантингдон, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon было 68 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda of Chester, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord Robert De Bruce в 1209 г. в местности хантингдон, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1251 г. в местности хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности савтри, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Isabel De Huntingdon nació en 1212, en Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, tenía 68 años y su mamá, Matilda of Chester, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Lord Robert De Bruce en 1209, en Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1251, en Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
